hotel.name: Long Beach Cha-am Hotel
hotel.description: A popular family beach hotel at the quieter northern end of Cha-am's beach strip. Large pool, beach access, well-maintained bungalows, and a restaurant. The northern end of Cha-am is less crowded and more family-friendly than the central beach area. Good value proposition with reliable facilities and consistent reviews.
highlights: Quieter northern Cha-am location with less beach hawker activity

similarHotels.name: Swiss Palazzo Hotel
similarHotels.description: A well-designed mid-range hotel located next to Phra Nakhon Khiri Historical Park with modern rooms, free Wi-Fi, and a rooftop garden overlooking the hilltop palace. The rooftop terrace with Khao Wang views is a genuine draw — breakfast with that backdrop makes the slightly higher price worthwhile. Clean, contemporary rooms with good beds and air conditioning.
highlights: Rooftop garden with Khao Wang Palace views
Located directly next to Phra Nakhon Khiri Historical Park entrance
Modern rooms with reliable AC and Wi-Fi

similarHotels.name: Fisherman's Resort Cha-am
similarHotels.description: A comfortable mid-range beach resort on Cha-am's central beachfront with a good-sized swimming pool, direct beach access, and cheerful Thai-style bungalow rooms and garden villas. Popular with Thai families on weekends. The beachfront location and pool make it noticeably better value than equivalent properties in Hua Hin next door. On-site seafood restaurant with fresh catch at reasonable prices.
highlights: Direct beach access on Cha-am's central beach
On-site restaurant with fresh Gulf seafood

similarHotels.name: Cha-am Methavalai Hotel
similarHotels.description: One of the most established hotels on Cha-am's beachfront strip, with a long stretch of private beach, two swimming pools, and spacious rooms with sea views. The hotel has a classic beach-resort feel — relaxed service, palm-shaded poolside loungers, and direct beach access. A solid mid-range option that offers genuine beach holiday value in the Phetchaburi province at significantly lower prices than comparable Hua Hin resorts.
highlights: Private beachfront on Cha-am's main beach strip
Two swimming pools and beach lounge chairs
Spacious sea-view rooms at mid-range prices

aiContent.hookIntro: Booking Long Beach Cha-am Hotel without knowing its northern end location can leave you isolated from Cha-am's central dining and nightlife scenes.
aiContent.intro: Long Beach Cha-am Hotel offers a relaxed family stay with direct beach access, a large pool, and well-maintained bungalow-style rooms in a quieter spot. It's ideal for families seeking longer beach stays away from crowds.
whyBook: Quieter northern Cha-am spot with minimal beach hawkers
Direct access to family-friendly beach area
Spacious bungalow rooms for comfortable stays
Large pool perfect for kids and relaxation
Reliable on-site restaurant for easy meals
whyNotBook: Northern location requires transport to central Cha-am attractions
Bungalows may feel dated compared to modern resorts
Limited evening entertainment on quieter end of beach
Pool area lacks shade options during peak sun hours
No nearby nightlife or shopping within walking distance
aiContent.skipIf: Skip if you're traveling solo or seeking vibrant central beach energy and easy access to Cha-am's main strip.
aiContent.detailedReview: Checking into Long Beach Cha-am Hotel feels straightforward and welcoming, with staff handling the process efficiently at the front desk near the large pool area. The bungalow-style rooms provide a cozy, home-like vibe with basic furnishings, ample space for families, and private patios that open toward the beach or garden—well-maintained but not overly modern, emphasizing comfort over luxury. The standout amenity is the expansive pool, surrounded by loungers and suitable for kids to splash around while parents relax nearby. Beach access is just steps away, leading to a serene stretch of sand ideal for family walks or building sandcastles, though it's less lively than central areas. The on-site restaurant serves straightforward Thai and Western dishes with fresh seafood options, maintaining consistent quality without gourmet flair—portions are generous, and it's convenient for breakfast or casual dinners. Service style is friendly and attentive, with staff often checking on families' needs, reflecting the hotel's family-oriented setup. Typical guests are parents with children or couples opting for extended beach retreats, drawn to the peaceful northern end of Cha-am where hawker activity is minimal. Overall, it delivers reliable value for those prioritizing tranquility and basic facilities over high-end perks, making multi-night stays enjoyable without surprises.
aiContent.locationReality: Situated at the northern end of Cha-am Beach in Phetchaburi, the hotel offers immediate walking access to the quieter beachfront, about 10-15 minutes by car from central Cha-am's busier strip. Phra Nakhon Khiri Historical Park is roughly a 20-minute drive north, while the main Phetchaburi town center requires a 30-40 minute Grab ride costing around 300-400 THB. Within a 5-minute walk, you'll find the beach and some local food stalls, but supermarkets or major restaurants need a short scooter or taxi trip.
localSignals: Direct beach access from property, no walking required
10-15 minute drive to central Cha-am beach strip
One large pool and one on-site restaurant available
20-minute drive to Phra Nakhon Khiri Historical Park
Nearest 7-Eleven within 5-minute walk along beach road
aiContent.travellerNote: Guests often praise the quiet family-friendly atmosphere and large pool but note the distance to central Cha-am's activities as a drawback for shorter trips.
bookingTips: Request bungalow rooms with beach views for better access
Check Agoda for lower rates than Booking.com in Thailand
Prices spike December to February during high season
Verify breakfast inclusion as it's not always standard
Book early for poolside rooms during school holidays

similarHotels.howDifferent: Offers modern rooms and rooftop garden near historical park, unlike the beach-focused bungalows at Long Beach Cha-am.

similarHotels.name: Fisherman's Resort Cha-am (1,500-2,800 THB/night (~$42-78 USD)) — A comfortable mid-range beach resort on Cha-am's central beachfront with a good-sized swimming pool, direct beach access, and cheerful Thai-
similarHotels.howDifferent: Provides central beachfront location with more vibrant surroundings, contrasting the quieter northern end of Long Beach Cha-am.

similarHotels.name: Cha-am Methavalai Hotel (1,800-3,200 THB/night (~$50-90 USD)) — One of the most established hotels on Cha-am's beachfront strip, with a long stretch of private beach, two swimming pools, and spacious room
similarHotels.howDifferent: Features two pools and private beach in a more established central spot, differing from the single-pool northern setup.

faq.q: Is Long Beach Cha-am Hotel suitable for families?
faq.a: Yes, it offers family-friendly facilities like a large pool and direct beach access in a quieter area ideal for kids.

faq.q: What amenities does Long Beach Cha-am Hotel have?
faq.a: Key amenities include a large pool, well-maintained bungalows, beach access, and an on-site restaurant.

faq.q: How far is Long Beach Cha-am Hotel from central Cha-am?
faq.a: It's at the northern end, about 10-15 minutes by car from the central beach strip.

faq.q: Does Long Beach Cha-am Hotel have beach access?
faq.a: Yes, guests have direct access to Cha-am Beach right from the property.

faq.q: Is the area around Long Beach Cha-am Hotel quiet?
faq.a: The northern end features less crowd and hawker activity, making it more peaceful than central Cha-am.

faq.q: What room types are available at Long Beach Cha-am Hotel?
faq.a: Primarily bungalow-style rooms designed for comfort, suitable for families and longer stays.

aiContent.urgencyLine: Rooms at Long Beach Cha-am Hotel with direct beach access book out 1-2 months ahead for high season—check today.
aiContent.methodology: We rated Long Beach Cha-am Hotel based on location, amenities, and value from provided data. We weighted family-friendly facilities highest because the hotel targets longer beach stays for groups. Prices and availability change—always verify before booking. Last updated: 2026-04.
aiContent.metaDescription: Long Beach Cha-am Hotel in Phetchaburi delivers quiet family beach stays with large pool and bungalows, but northern spot means drives to central action. Mid-range value at 2,000-3,500 THB/night—book for relaxed escapes.
